title: Connectivity applications

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Connectivity applications; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Connectivity applications
sources.claim: Disjoint-set supports incremental undirected connectivity, Kruskal cycle checks, grouping, and equivalence merging.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Disjoint-set supports incremental undirected connectivity, Kruskal cycle checks, grouping, and equivalence merging.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Adding an undirected edge forms a cycle exactly when its endpoints already share a representative. Your goal is to solve and test a connectivity problem using the disjoint-set API. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Tree terminology and representation; Array representation and operations; Path compression; Graph terminology and models. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Disjoint-set supports incremental undirected connectivity, Kruskal cycle checks, grouping, and equivalence merging. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: It does not support arbitrary deletions or directed reachability without additional algorithms. The draft therefore rejects: Disjoint-set can answer the actual path between two connected vertices.

includes: Use disjoint sets for incremental connectivity, cycle checks and spanning-tree construction.
Solve and test a connectivity problem using the disjoint-set API.

In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.
excludes: It does not support arbitrary deletions or directed reachability without additional algorithms.

outcomes: Solve and test a connectivity problem using the disjoint-set API.

examples.title: Connectivity applications: language-neutral trace
examples.situation: Adding an undirected edge forms a cycle exactly when its endpoints already share a representative.
walkthrough: Step 1 — State the abstract input and invariant: Adding an undirected edge forms a cycle exactly when its endpoints already share a representative.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Disjoint-set answers whether sets are connected, not which path connects their members.
examples.result: The conceptual result follows because Disjoint-set answers whether sets are connected, not which path connects their members.

examples.title: Connectivity applications: implementation transfer
examples.situation: In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Keep the same specification and invariant across all four languages.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: It does not support arbitrary deletions or directed reachability without additional algorithms.
examples.result: Concept mastery transfers, while implementation evidence remains language-specific.
